findings.title: DIRECTIVE-20260524-001 handled: PROP-054 authored
findings.evidence: Pending directive (priority medium, filed 2026-05-24T08:15Z by operator-via-cowork-claude) requested PROP-NNN authoring for the prune-integrity.js mtime-vs-filename bug. This run: (a) verified the bug empirically (every checked-out file has mtime=clone-time 2026-05-24T06:12:08Z), (b) measured the actual eligible-after-fix count (249 today, with workspace-sync-runs entering the eligible set in ~2 days as oldest crosses 30d), (c) designed parseFilenameTimestamp helper with 9 patterns covering 1,156/1,156 actual files (100%) plus 5 adversarial false-positive tests, (d) added keep_last_n safety floors per directive operator_preference, (e) phased rollout per directive (dry-run current → dry-run fixed → cap-apply at --max-archive-per-policy 100 → full apply), (f) addressed the 4 counter-arguments enumerated in the directive task_4. PROP-054 status: pending-operator-review.

findings.title: Directive's '~700 eligible after fix' estimate vs measured 249
findings.evidence: The directive estimated ~700 of ~1,149 retention-policy files would become eligible after the fix lands. Empirical measurement against the live clone (commit 075e090) shows 249 eligible today (175 verify-pending-run + 74 push-failure). The difference is workspace-sync-runs: the directive assumed all 570 were past the 30-day cutoff, but the oldest is 27.6d. Within 2-3 days, workspace-sync-runs starts entering the eligible set; report-daily starts in ~42 days. The directive's estimate becomes accurate over the rollout window, but on day-zero apply the eligible count is ~1/3 of the directive's number. PROP-054 documents this reconciliation transparently in executive_summary and phase_4_design.impact_estimate.

props_authored_this_run.title: prune-integrity.js: parse filename-embedded timestamps instead of fs.statSync().mtimeMs

claude_md_audit.reason: Directive-override run; CLAUDE.md accuracy/performance audit deferred to next Mode 3 run. No CLAUDE.md changes required by PROP-054 — the prune-integrity.js script is documented in PROP-051 and CLAUDE.md mentions Workstream C generically.

operator_action_summary: If approved: apply patch to monitor/scripts/prune-integrity.js per phase_1_design + phase_2_design code blocks, then commit via /tmp/edit-clone or push-via-api.js.
Run phase_5_rollout step 1 (dry-run current logic, baseline) and step 2 (dry-run fixed logic, verify ~249 eligible) before stepping to step 3 (apply with --max-archive-per-policy 100).
After the next scheduled dome-prune-integrity run produces a clean capped apply, drop the cap (step 4).
Mark DIRECTIVE-20260524-001 → already done by this run (status: completed). No further directive housekeeping needed.
